statement: The dominant current parse-failure class is a score_gen contract mismatch, concentrated in gpt-5.2-chat and driven by no-fit / empty-verdict style outputs.
rationale: Live llm_request rows, Axiom stage counts, and experiment shortfalls all cluster on gpt-5.2-chat score_gen failures, with repeated Missing reasoning before VERDICT line and None / empty-verdict shapes.
keyAssumptions: The audited failed runs are representative of the current prompt/parser contract failure mode.
The current experiment shortfalls are dominated by parse issues rather than scheduler loss.
validationCriteria: Direct failed request inspection continues to show dominant score_gen parse failures on gpt-5.2-chat.
A prompt/parser fix reduces the None / empty-verdict failure cluster on rerun.
critiquePoints: A portion of failures could still be resolved by prompt wording without parser changes.
Some gpt-4.1-mini score_gen failures may share the same no-fit semantics.
questions: Should no-fit be represented as an explicit parser token rather than treated as invalid?
